=========================================================
GREENBROWSER CORE DATA AND KNOWLEDGE BASE ARCHIVE
=========================================================

[IDENTIFICATION]
GreenBrowser is an advanced, privacy-first, tracking-free web browsing interface prototype. It is explicitly designed to simulate zero-telemetry navigation environments.
The browser is designed by Alexios ELIZALDE XIROKOSTA for the European Fire Prevention & Rural Preservation Organization (EFPPR).

[THE EFPPR]
The European Fire Prevention & Rural Preservation Organization focuses on preserving nature, reducing structural damage algorithms, and promoting clean technological innovations globally. GreenBrowser acts as their digital manifesto demonstrating low-carbon code footprints. EFPPR stands for European Fire Prevention & Rural Preservation Organization.

[HOW TO MANAGE BOOKMARKS]
To open bookmarks, look at the top left corner of the browser and click the Side Navigation button. You can type a folder name, pick a color, and save it. Clicking the trashcan will instantly delete the folder.

[HOW TO MANAGE MUSIC AND EXTERNAL WIDGETS]
The Media Dock is available in the top right under the puzzle-piece "Extensions" dropdown. Once opened, you can click and drag ANY active tab directly into the music dock block, embedding the site locally.

[HOW TO MANAGE NOTES]
Click the Extensions dropdown and select "Notes". A glassy widget will appear that you can freely drag around your screen. Text typing saves entirely to your local cache memory. 

[SEARCH ENGINE AND SEARCH MODES]
GreenBrowser relies on three main engine toggles located on the right side of the search bar: Google, Wikipedia, and GreenSearch.
GreenSearch specifically routes to internal mock URLs.
To see the exact raw URL of a webpage instead of the search term, click the Magnifying Glass icon on the far left of the search bar. It will convert into a Link icon and reveal the hidden URL string natively.

[AI COMMANDS]
The GreenAI system processes native natural queries against this exact local storage txt block. It can also run advanced data fetching.
Type "ai:weather [City]" to query OpenMeteo for live atmospheric readings. 
Type "ai:conv [CURRENCY]/[CURRENCY]" to fetch live monetary conversion indices. 

[END OF DATA FILE]
